concepts.name: TotalRevenue
concepts.description: Total top-line revenue recognized during the period. Represents the aggregate net sales of goods and services before any deductions for costs or expenses. Segment sub-totals, deferred revenue balances, and revenue-adjacent items are excluded to prevent double-counting.

concepts.name: CostOfRevenue
concepts.description: Direct costs attributable to the production of goods sold or services delivered. Includes raw materials, direct labor, and manufacturing overhead. Excludes operating expenses such as SG&A and R&D.

concepts.name: GrossProfit
concepts.description: Revenue minus Cost of Revenue. Represents the profit a company earns after subtracting the direct costs of producing its products or delivering its services, before operating expenses, interest, and taxes.

concepts.name: OperatingExpenses
concepts.description: Total operating expenses incurred in the ordinary course of business, including SG&A, R&D, and other recurring operational costs. Excludes non-operating items such as interest expense and income tax.

concepts.name: ResearchAndDevelopment
concepts.description: Costs incurred in the research and development of new products, technologies, or processes. A forward-looking investment indicator — high R&D as a percentage of revenue often signals innovation-driven growth strategies.

concepts.name: SellingGeneralAdmin
concepts.description: Selling, General and Administrative expenses — the overhead costs of running the business not directly tied to production. Includes marketing, executive compensation, rent, legal, and other administrative costs.

concepts.name: OperatingIncome
concepts.description: Earnings from core business operations before interest income/expense and income taxes (EBIT proxy). Calculated as Gross Profit minus Operating Expenses. A key measure of operational efficiency independent of capital structure.

concepts.name: NetIncome
concepts.description: The bottom-line profit attributable to the consolidated entity after all expenses, taxes, interest, and non-controlling interest deductions. Comprehensive income adjustments and per-share variants are excluded.

concepts.name: EPS_Basic
concepts.description: Basic Earnings Per Share — net income divided by the weighted-average number of common shares outstanding during the period, excluding dilutive securities such as stock options and convertible instruments.

concepts.name: EPS_Diluted
concepts.description: Diluted Earnings Per Share — net income divided by the weighted-average diluted share count, which includes all potentially dilutive securities (options, warrants, convertible bonds). The most conservative EPS measure and the standard for valuation multiples.

concepts.name: CashAndEquivalents
concepts.description: Cash and short-term liquid instruments with original maturities of three months or less, including bank deposits and money market funds. Includes restricted cash where the SEC-mandated combined presentation is reported.

concepts.name: TotalAssets
concepts.description: The aggregate of all assets owned or controlled by the entity — current assets, property and equipment, intangible assets, and other long-term holdings. Equals total liabilities plus stockholders' equity (the fundamental accounting identity). Asset sub-categories and segment breakdowns are excluded.

concepts.name: CurrentAssets
concepts.description: Assets expected to be converted to cash or consumed within one operating cycle (typically 12 months). Includes cash, accounts receivable, inventory, and short-term investments. A key component of liquidity analysis.

concepts.name: TotalLiabilities
concepts.description: The total of all financial obligations owed by the entity — both current (due within 12 months) and long-term. Includes debt, accounts payable, deferred revenue, and other commitments.

concepts.name: TotalLiabilitiesAndEquity
concepts.description: The sum of total liabilities and stockholders' equity — the right-hand side of the balance sheet. Must equal Total Assets by the fundamental accounting identity (Assets = Liabilities + Equity). Used as a cross-check for balance sheet integrity.

concepts.name: StockholdersEquity
concepts.description: The residual interest in the entity's assets after deducting all liabilities — the book value owned by shareholders. Includes paid-in capital, retained earnings, accumulated other comprehensive income, and non-controlling interests where reported on a consolidated basis.

concepts.name: OperatingCashFlow
concepts.description: Net cash generated or consumed by the company's core operating activities during the period. Often considered more reliable than net income as a measure of underlying business profitability because it is harder to manipulate with non-cash accounting choices.

concepts.name: InvestingCashFlow
concepts.description: Net cash used in or generated by investing activities — primarily capital expenditures (outflow), acquisitions, and proceeds from asset sales or investment maturities (inflows). A consistently negative value typically indicates a capital-intensive growth strategy.

concepts.name: FinancingCashFlow
concepts.description: Net cash from financing activities — debt issuance and repayments, equity raises, share buybacks, and dividend payments. Reflects how the company funds its operations and returns capital to shareholders.

concepts.name: CAPEX
concepts.description: Capital Expenditures — cash paid to acquire, maintain, or upgrade physical assets such as property, plant, and equipment. Key input for Free Cash Flow calculations: FCF = OperatingCashFlow − CAPEX.
